place: Troy Hills CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: The name "Parsippany" comes from the Lenape word "Parseponong," meaning "the place where the waters come together," a nod to the local streams.
Troy Hills Historic District, with 15 homes dating from 1720 to 1920, was added to the New Jersey Register of Historic Places in 2015.
European settlers first arrived in the area around 1715, establishing Troy Village by 1720 along Troy Brook.
The township that now contains Troy Hills was incorporated in 1928 from parts of Hanover Township, shifting from farms to suburban neighborhoods after WWII.
Archaeological digs at the Dale Road rockshelter reveal summer camps from 6,000 years ago, showing the Lenni‑Lenape used the area for hunting and fishing.
